ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Rajma. Nearest parent is a cooked kidney bean dish. A new concept for Indian kidney bean curry is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cooked kidney bean curry, North Indian style (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of rajma is 1 bowl (approximately 200g) — a thick, hearty, dark red kidney bean curry with a robust onion-tomato gravy, served classically with steamed basmati rice (Rajma-Chawal).

nutrient_source.title: Rajma — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, derived from cooked kidney bean composition data and standard rajma recipe
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN does not have a standalone rajma curry entry. Values derived from ICMR-NIN cooked kidney bean data and standard rajma recipe proportions (kidney beans ~55%, tomato/onion gravy ~35%, oil ~5%, spices balance).

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — kidney beans GI approximately 29; whole legume structure gives very low GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 536mg per bowl (200g) — moderate to elevated; high potassium (636mg per bowl) and magnesium from kidney beans provide significant blood pressure benefit.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients — the high potassium and magnesium from kidney beans are highly beneficial for blood pressure management. Reduce added salt during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~29); glycaemic load per bowl is approximately 12. The very high dietary fibre (6.8g/100g), resistant starch, and protein of kidney beans provide outstanding glycaemic control.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rajma is highly recommended for diabetic patients — kidney beans are one of the most diabetes-friendly foods. Regular consumption is associated with improved HbA1c and fasting glucose.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Kidney beans provide 88mcg DFE folate per 100g — one of the highest folate foods; folate supports cell repair and DNA synthesis important for immune function.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Highly suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. The exceptional folate content supports immune modulation; magnesium and phosphorus support bone health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rajma is a beloved North Indian and Punjabi comfort dish of plump, pressure-cooked red kidney beans simmered in a thick, flavourful onion-tomato gravy spiced with cumin, coriander, garam masala, and ginger-garlic paste. The curry is thicker and more robust than dal makhani. Classically paired with plain steamed basmati rice in the iconic Rajma-Chawal combination — one of the most satisfying and nutritionally complete meals in Indian home cooking. Vegan, gluten-free, and exceptionally high in fibre, protein, folate, and potassium.

confidence_reason: No standalone rajma entry in ICMR-NIN or HPB Foodo. Values derived from ICMR-NIN cooked kidney bean composition data and standard recipe. Kidney bean nutritional values are well-established; estimates are reliable.
notes: Vegan and gluten-free. Cholesterol is 0.0. One of the highest fibre dishes in Indian cuisine (6.8g/100g). Kidney beans must be properly soaked and pressure-cooked to deactivate lectins. The Rajma-Chawal combination (rajma + steamed rice) significantly increases total calories and carbohydrate content vs the curry alone.
